signal_title: European Commission's Guidance on Age Assurance
risk_rationale: The European Commission's guidance on age assurance introduces new requirements for third-party verification and registration processes that are not currently addressed in TikTok's internal baseline, which relies on machine learning and telecom partnerships for age estimation.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: TikTok states it uses machine learning and safety review to identify likely under-13 users and remove/limit access.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Third-party verification and registration processes for age assurance.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current baseline does not include third-party verification or registration processes for age assurance as required by the new EU guidance.

remediations.title: Implement Third-Party Age Verification
remediations.description: Integrate third-party age verification services to comply with EU guidance on age assurance, ensuring transparency and effectiveness in protecting minors.
acceptance_criteria: Integration with at least one third-party age verification service
Updated user registration process to include third-party verification
Compliance with EU transparency requirements for age assurance

business_impact: Failure to comply with the new EU guidance on age assurance could result in regulatory scrutiny and potential fines, impacting TikTok's operations in the EU market.
recommended_actions: Engage with third-party age verification providers to explore integration options.
Update the user registration process to incorporate third-party verification.
Ensure transparency in age assurance methods to align with EU guidance.
